tool.name: SOPA Segmentation
tool.command:
@CMD_UNZIP_ZARR@
mkdir -p output_dir &&
_run_seg() { PATCH_FLAG="\${1:+--patch-index \$1}" sopa segmentation #if $method.method_name == 'cellpose' cellpose input/spatialdata.zarr --diameter $method.diameter #for $channel in str($method.channels).split(',') --channels '$channel' #end for --flow-threshold $method.flow_threshold --cellprob-threshold $method.cellprob_threshold --model-type '$method.model_type' #if $method.pretrained_model --pretrained-model '$method.pretrained_model' #end if --min-area $method.min_area @CMD_IMG_PREPROCESSING@
#else if $method.method_name == 'stardist' stardist input/spatialdata.zarr --model-type '$method.model_type' #if $method.local_model --local-model '$method.local_model' #end if --prob-thresh $method.prob_thresh --nms-thresh $method.nms_thresh #if str($method.channels) != '' --channels '$method.channels' #end if --min-area $method.min_area @CMD_IMG_PREPROCESSING@
#else if $method.method_name == 'comseg' comseg input/spatialdata.zarr --min-area $method.min_area
#else if $method.method_name == 'baysor' baysor input/spatialdata.zarr --min-area $method.min_area #if $method.scale --scale $method.scale #end if
#else if $method.method_name == 'proseg' proseg input/spatialdata.zarr $method.infer_presets #if str($method.prior_shapes_key) != '' --prior-shapes-key '$method.prior_shapes_key' #end if #if str($method.bins_key) != '' --bins-key '$method.bins_key' #end if
#else if $method.method_name == 'tissue' tissue input/spatialdata.zarr #if str($method.image_key) != '' --image-key '$method.image_key' #end if --level $method.level --mode '$method.mode' --kwargs { "expand_radius_ratio": $method.expand_radius_ratio, #if str($method.channel) != '' "channel": '$method.channel', #end if "clip_parameters": [$method.clip_parameters], "blur_kernel_size": $method.blur_kernel_size, "open_kernel_size": $method.open_kernel_size, "close_kernel_size": $method.close_kernel_size, "drop_threshold": $method.drop_threshold, "allow_holes": $method.allow_holes }
#end if \$PATCH_FLAG } &&
#if $method.method_name == 'proseg' or $method.method_name == 'tissue' echo "Running segmentation (no patch support)" && _run_seg #else NUM_PATCHES=$(python -c "import spatialdata as sd; s = sd.read_zarr('input/spatialdata.zarr'); print(s.shapes['image_patches'].shape[0] if 'image_patches' in s.shapes else '')") && if [ -n "\$NUM_PATCHES" ]; then echo "Found \$NUM_PATCHES patches, running per-patch segmentation" && for PATCH_IDX in \$(seq 0 \$((NUM_PATCHES - 1))); do echo "Processing patch \$PATCH_IDX/\$((NUM_PATCHES - 1))" && _run_seg \$PATCH_IDX && done && echo "Resolving patch conflicts" && sopa resolve #if $method.method_name == 'cellpose' cellpose #else if $method.method_name == 'stardist' stardist #else if $method.method_name == 'baysor' baysor --min-area $method.min_area #else if $method.method_name == 'comseg' comseg --min-area $method.min_area #end if input/spatialdata.zarr else echo "No patches found, running segmentation on whole data" && _run_seg fi #end if &&
## Move to output directory mv "input/spatialdata.zarr" output_dir/ &&
@CMD_ZIP_ZARR@

param.name: method_name
param.label: Segmentation method
param.option: Cellpose - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Stardist - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Comseg - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Baysor - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Proseg - This needs to be done on a single patch as proseg is fast enough and doesn't require parallelization.
Tissue - This can be done only on objects with H&E staining

param.label: Expected cell diameter (pixels)
param.help: If 0, the diameter will be automatically estimated. However, providing a value can improve results.

param.label: Channel names (comma-separated)
param.help: If one channel, then provide just a nucleus channel. If two channels, this is the cytoplasm and then the nucleus channel

param.label: Flow threshold
param.help: Increase this threshold if cellpose is not returning as many ROIs as you would expect. Similarly, decrease this threshold if cellpose is returning too many ill-shaped ROIs.

param.label: Cell probability threshold
param.help: Decrease this threshold if cellpose is not returning as many ROIs as you would expect. Similarly, increase this threshold if cellpose is returning too ROIs particularly from dim areas.

param.label: The typical cell radius in microns
param.help: The configuration is inferred based on this parameter.

param.label: infer the proseg presets based on the columns of the transcripts dataframe?
Key of spdata object containing the prior cell boundaries [Only for Visium HD data]
param.help: If `'auto'`, use the latest performed segmentation (e.g., stardist or the 10X Genomics segmentation).

param.label: Key of spdata object containing the bin-by-gene table of gene counts [Only for Visium HD data]
Name of the image key to use for tissue segmentation
Level of the multiscale image to use for tissue segmentation
The ratio of the radius of the polygons that will be expanded.
Channel
param.help: The channel to use for the staining mode. If None, the maximum value of all channels is used.

param.label: Clip parameters
param.help: Parameters used to get the threshold used to clip the image before converting it to an 8-bit image (only used in 'staining' mode). The first parameter is the quantile, and the second is the divisor. By default, the threshold is the 90th quantile divided by 5.

param.label: The kernel size of the median blurring operation
The kernel size of the morphological opening operation.
The kernel size of the morphological closing operation.
Drop threshold
param.help: Polygons whose area is inferior to drop_threshold * total_slide_area will be removed.

param.label: Allow holes
param.help: If yes, the holes in the polygons will be kept. If no, the holes will be removed.

tool.help:
**What it does**
SOPA Segmentation performs cell segmentation on patched spatial omics data. It supports multiple segmentation methods:
1. **Cellpose**: Deep learning-based segmentation for fluorescence images (most common) 2. **Baysor**: Transcript-based segmentation for spatial transcriptomics 3. **Generic staining-based**: Custom segmentation methods (e.g., StarDist)
**Requirements**
- Input must be a patched SpatialData object (output from SOPA Patchify tool) - For Cellpose: Image data with at least one channel (e.g., DAPI) - For Baysor: Transcript-level data with spatial coordinates
**Cellpose Parameters**
Critical parameters that vary by technology:
**Diameter (pixels)** - Typical cell diameter: - Xenium: 50-70 pixels - MERSCOPE: Not applicable (use Baysor) - CosMx: 40-60 pixels - Visium HD: 50-80 pixels - MACSima/PhenoCycler: 40-60 pixels
**Min area (pixels²)** - Minimum cell size: - General: 2000-5000 pixels² - Adjust based on expected cell sizes in your tissue
**Model selection:** - **cyto3** (recommended): Latest general-purpose cytoplasm model - **nuclei**: For nucleus-only segmentation - **Custom model**: Upload your own trained Cellpose model
**Advanced options:** - **Gaussian sigma**: Smooth noisy/pixelated images (try 2.0) - **CLAHE clip limit**: Enhance contrast in dim images (try 0.3) - **Resample**: Disable for faster processing at cost of quality
**Baysor Configuration**
Baysor requires a TOML configuration file with parameters like: - `scale`: Typical cell radius in microns (IMPORTANT) - `min_molecules_per_cell`: Minimum transcripts per cell - `n_clusters`: Number of cell types (optional)
See `SOPA documentation <https://prism-oncology.github.io/sopa/tutorials/cli_usage/#option-2-baysor>`_ for example configurations.
**Performance Notes**
- Segmentation can be time-consuming for large datasets - For very large images, consider using smaller patches or reducing patch overlap - Cellpose with GPU acceleration is much faster than CPU-only (Galaxy admin configuration)
**Troubleshooting**
**Too few cells detected:** - Decrease diameter parameter - Lower flow_threshold (e.g., 1.5) and cellprob_threshold (e.g., -8.0) - Check min_area isn't too restrictive
**Too many false positives:** - Increase diameter parameter - Increase flow_threshold and cellprob_threshold - Increase min_area to filter small artifacts
**More Information**
See the `SOPA segmentation documentation <https://prism-oncology.github.io/sopa/tutorials/cli_usage/#run-segmentation>`_.
